label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know which statement is true about folk medicine based on the provided passage. Let me read through the information again to make sure I understand it correctly. The passage starts by explaining that folk medicine uses plants and herbs from the surrounding environment to keep the body healthy. It mentions that since the materials are available everywhere, anyone can practice it anywhere. Then it talks about how the use of folk medicine dates back to the earliest times when both humans and animals used plants and herbs to stay healthy. It gives examples of animals using snakeroot after a snake bite and how humans might have learned from observing animals. The passage notes that animals rest and seek cool places when sick, and humans observed this. It also mentions that some people think folk medicine includes old wives' tales, like hanging a vegetable around the neck for health, but those are fairy tales and separate from actual folk medicine. Now, looking at the options: A) It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs. B) It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals. C) It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases. D) It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history. The passage clearly states that folk medicine uses plants and herbs to keep the body healthy, which matches option A. The first sentence says, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." So A seems correct. Option B: The passage says folk medicine was used by both man and animals, but does it say it can treat "most kinds of diseases"? The example given is for snake bites and fevers, but the passage doesn't make a broad claim about most diseases. Also, the part about animals using snakeroot is presented as an example, not a comprehensive statement. So B might be an overstatement. Option C: The passage does not compare folk medicine to other types of medicine in terms of effectiveness for serious diseases. It doesn't say it's better, so C is probably incorrect. Option D: The passage says folk medicine goes back to the earliest times but doesn't state it was used only then. In fact, the first sentence suggests it's still practiced today ("one can practise folk medicine in any place of the world"). So D is incorrect because "only in man's early history" is too restrictive. The key here is that A is directly supported by the passage's opening statement, while the other options either go beyond what's stated
